summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Gustavo Borges landing page (dark+gold theme). All 8 backend pytest cases pass (stats GET/PUT, leads POST/GET, no _id leak). Frontend loads cleanly, all sections render, all data-testid attributes present, all CTAs point to correct WhatsApp/Instagram URLs, portfolio cards link to the 3 Netlify projects, animated counter shows +10 on scroll, niches marquee shows default 4 niches.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: ensure_stats() is idempotent and safe, sites_delivered cannot go negative due to max(0, ...). Good.

CORS allow_credentials=True combined with allow_origins='*' is technically invalid per CORS spec; browsers may ignore credentials. Not an issue for this app (no auth/cookies) but worth a fix if auth is added.
Proof.jsx: counter starts from 0 and animates to target on intersection — good UX.

Portfolio.jsx: iframes use sandbox='allow-same-origin allow-scripts' which is appropriate. External iframe apps log their own console errors (Base44Error, createAxiosClient) — these come from the iframed Netlify sites, NOT from this app, and cannot be fixed here.
App.js: silent catch on /api/stats failure with fallback defaults — resilient. Consider logging errors in dev for debuggability.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: None. Backend auto-seeds default Stats (sites_delivered=10, 4 default niches) via ensure_stats() on first call and on startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. All 8 tests pass. Iframe portfolio previews may emit console errors from external Netlify apps — these are third-party and should be ignored. Counter animates from 0 to stats.sites_delivered on scroll into Proof section.
